rules.name: Direct Variable in SQL Query
rules.description: Variable directly embedded in SQL query without parameterized statement
rules.recommendation: Use prepared statements with parameterized queries. Never concatenate user input directly into SQL queries.
remediationSteps: Use prepared statements (prepared/parameterized queries)
Use parameterized queries with placeholders (?)
Implement input validation and whitelist known safe values
Use ORM frameworks that handle parameterization automatically

rules.name: SQL Injection via String Concatenation
rules.description: User input concatenated with SQL query using dot operator
rules.recommendation: Use stored procedures or prepared statements instead of string concatenation
remediationSteps: Replace concatenation with prepared statements
Use PDO with parameterized queries
Implement whitelist validation for known safe columns/tables
Use database abstraction layers

rules.name: mysqli_query with Unsanitized Input
rules.description: mysqli_query used with user input without proper escaping or parameterization
rules.recommendation: Use prepared statements with mysqli instead of directly passing user input to mysqli_query
remediationSteps: Use $conn->prepare() for prepared statements
Use bind_param() to bind user input to placeholders
Execute the prepared statement
Fetch results using get_result() or bind_result()

rules.name: mysql_query with Unsanitized Input (Deprecated)
rules.description: Deprecated mysql_query() used with user input. This extension was removed in PHP 7.0
rules.secureExample: // Use MySQLi or PDO instead - mysql_* functions are deprecated
rules.recommendation: Migrate from deprecated mysql_* functions to MySQLi or PDO with prepared statements
remediationSteps: Replace mysql_* functions with mysqli_* or PDO equivalents
Use prepared statements exclusively
Update database connection code
Test all database queries thoroughly

rules.name: PDO Query without Prepared Statement
rules.description: PDO query() method used instead of prepare() for parameterized queries
rules.recommendation: Use PDO prepared statements with bindParam or bindValue instead of direct query()
remediationSteps: Use PDOStatement::prepare() to create a prepared statement
Use bindParam() for named or positional placeholders
Use execute() to run the prepared statement
Avoid using query() with user input

rules.name: SQL Variable Interpolation
rules.description: Variable interpolated directly inside double-quoted SQL string
rules.recommendation: Avoid variable interpolation in SQL strings. Use prepared statements instead
remediationSteps: Replace interpolated variables with parameterized placeholders
Use prepared statements with bind_param() or bindValue()
Utilize named parameters for better readability
Validate input type (is it numeric? string? etc)

rules.name: SQL Injection in WHERE Clause
rules.description: WHERE clause constructed with user input without parameterization
rules.recommendation: Always use parameterized queries for WHERE clauses
remediationSteps: Use prepared statements for WHERE clause conditions
Bind user input to parameterized placeholders
Never concatenate user input into WHERE conditions
Implement input validation (whitelist approach)

rules.name: SQL Injection in ORDER BY
rules.description: ORDER BY clause accepts user input without validation
rules.recommendation: Use a whitelist of allowed columns for ORDER BY instead of user input directly
remediationSteps: Create a whitelist of allowed column names
Validate that user input matches one of the allowed columns
Construct ORDER BY using the validated column name
Use prepared statements when user input must be used dynamically

rules.name: SQL Injection in LIKE Clause
rules.description: LIKE clause with unsanitized user input allowing SQL injection
rules.recommendation: Use prepared statements with LIKE queries to prevent LIKE-based SQL injection
remediationSteps: Use prepared statements with LIKE placeholders
Properly escape special characters (%) with prepared statements
Always use bind_param or bindValue for LIKE searches
Consider using FULLTEXT search as alternative for large datasets

rules.name: SQL Injection via UNION-Based
rules.description: UNION SELECT pattern detected indicating SQL injection attempt
rules.vulnerableExample: User input: ' UNION SELECT username, password FROM admin--
rules.secureExample: Use prepared statements which prevent UNION-based attacks
rules.recommendation: Always use prepared statements which prevent UNION-based SQL injection
remediationSteps: Use prepared statements exclusively
Validate input against expected format
Implement WAF rules to detect UNION-based payloads
Log and alert on suspicious query patterns

metadata.description: SQL Injection (SQLi) detection ruleset for SecureCLI vulnerability scanner
